pages.text: Oluhle wakes up early. She is excited to get water. “Come Mama, we have to be first today,” she calls.

blocks.text: Oluhle wakes up early. She is excited to get water.
“Come Mama, we have to be first today,” she calls.

pages.text: On the way they meet Nathi and Unathi. They are going to the tap with their mother.

blocks.text: On the way they meet Nathi and Unathi. They are going to the tap with their mother.

pages.text: Oluhle and her friends play while the grown-ups get water. “Oluhle, your bucket is full,” says Mama.

blocks.text: Oluhle and her friends play while the grown-ups get water.
“Oluhle, your bucket is full,” says Mama.

pages.text: At home, Oluhle takes a bath. Mama goes to work and Oluhle stays with Makhulu.

blocks.text: At home, Oluhle takes a bath. Mama goes to work and Oluhle stays with Makhulu.

pages.text: In the afternoon Makhulu starts to make supper. She needs more water. She calls Oluhle so they can go to the tap.

blocks.text: In the afternoon Makhulu starts to make supper. She needs more water. She calls Oluhle so they can go to the tap.

pages.text: “Makhulu, I can’t find my bucket. I searched everywhere,” says Oluhle. Makhulu helps her look, but the bucket is nowhere to be found.

blocks.text: “Makhulu, I can’t find my bucket. I searched everywhere,” says Oluhle.
Makhulu helps her look, but the bucket is nowhere to be found.

pages.text: Makhulu thinks of a plan. She gets a 2-litre bottle from the cupboard and fetches paints and paint brushes.

blocks.text: Makhulu thinks of a plan. She gets a 2-litre bottle from the cupboard and fetches paints and paint brushes.

pages.text: Makhulu makes a bucket from the water bottle. She paints it pink. “Here’s a recycled bucket, Oluhle!”

blocks.text: Makhulu makes a bucket from the water bottle. She paints it pink. “Here’s a recycled bucket, Oluhle!”

pages.text: “Thank you, Makhulu,” says Oluhle. She is excited to go to the tap with her new recycled bucket.

blocks.text: “Thank you, Makhulu,” says Oluhle. She is excited to go to the tap with her new recycled bucket.

pages.text: The next day Unathi and Nathi bring two bottles to Makhulu. “Makhulu, could you please make recycled buckets for us?”

blocks.text: The next day Unathi and Nathi bring two bottles to Makhulu.
“Makhulu, could you please make recycled buckets for us?”

pages.text: Makhulu paints their buckets yellow and blue, their favourite colours. Nathi and Unathi are happy. Oluhle is happy too.

blocks.text: Makhulu paints their buckets yellow and blue, their favourite colours. Nathi and Unathi are happy. Oluhle is happy too.

pages.text: The friends can’t wait to show off with their new buckets. “Can we go to the tap now?” they ask.

blocks.text: The friends can’t wait to show off with their new buckets.
